description.value:
Source: Ariadne's Book of Legends - Alpha v1.2, p. 16.

Action Priority

The many features present in this book make it possible for several creatures to act outside of their turn simultaneously. Extra actions, Legendary Actions, Mythical Techniques, and certain class features can all compete for the same window between turns.

When two or more creatures attempt to use Legendary Actions or Extra actions at the end of the same turn, resolve them in initiative order. A 25th-level Fighter using their Legendary Action feature at the end of another player’s turn, for example, will act before a Legendary Foe’s Extra Action if the Fighter has the higher initiative count–and after it if they do not.

Some Mythical Techniques and class features can also be used outside of one’s turn by expending LP. These are resolved after Extra actions and Legendary Actions and follow the same initiative order priority.

Reactions and Phase Transitions are the exception to this rule. They resolve immediately when triggered, taking precedence over all other pending actions. If multiple reactions or Phase Transitions are triggered at the same time, resolve them in reverse order–the most recently triggered effect resolves first.

Action Priority

Priority Action Type Timing

1st Reactions & Phase Transitions

Immediately on trigger, most recent first

2nd Extra Actions & Legendary Actions

In initiative order, highest first

3rd All other out-of-turn features

In initiative order, highest first

source.custom: Original en-US text from Alpha v1.2
